elements.text: Ash falls like black snow through the midnight air. Below, the iron gate of the strongman's compound hangs from a single hinge, surrounded by the bodies of those who tried to flee.
They're tearing themselves to pieces down there. You didn't even have to lift a finger.
Kaelen stands on the ridge, his gaze fixed on the burning warehouses. The light of the fire flickers across his analytical, emotionless face.
A hierarchy built on leverage collapses the moment the leverage is redistributed. I merely altered their calculations.
Right. By convincing his lieutenants he was going to purge them. They did your dirty work for you.

In the courtyard below, the strongman stumbles out of his quarters, clutching a bleeding chest wound. He looks up at the ridge, screaming curses into the dark, before collapsing into the ash. He does not move again.
The vacuum is temporary. We must secure the distribution vaults before the neighboring districts realize the seat is empty.
And once we do? You think the others are just going to let you keep it? You've put a target on both our backs.
Let them look. Attention is merely another variable to be managed. Gather the keyholders who survived. They report to me now.
Renna hesitates, looking at the dead tyrant below, then at the silent, imposing figure of Kaelen. She nods slowly, stepping into the shadow he now casts over the district.
